hero.title: Dropdown Menu
hero.subtitle: Click the button, explore the menu, and discover the design intent behind each step.

basic.title: Open, close, and select
basic.description: The most essential behavior of a dropdown menu. Click the button to open, select an item to close. Clicking outside also closes it.
basic.hint: Click the button and select an item
basic.insight: The compound component pattern makes each sub-component's role explicit. Context silently provides state — no prop drilling needed.

pattern.title: Why compound components?
pattern.description: Compare two ways to build the same dropdown. A single component with all props vs. a compound component split by role. The code tells you why we chose composition.
pattern.insight: A single component is simple but rigid to customize. Compound components let you independently swap or extend each part — design-system-level flexibility.

strategy.title: Where should the menu float?
strategy.description: The same dropdown behaves differently depending on where it renders in the DOM. See the difference inside an overflow: hidden container.
strategy.hint: Open both dropdowns and compare the clipping
strategy.insight: Relative is simple but vulnerable to layout constraints. Portal is free from constraints but requires scroll/resize recalculation. Know your context to pick the right strategy.

keyboard.title: No mouse required
keyboard.description: Every interaction works with the keyboard alone. Disabled items are automatically skipped.
keyboard.hint: Tab to the button, Enter to open, arrow keys to navigate
keyboard.insight: Skipping disabled items isn't simple index math — it traverses a filtered array of enabled items in a cycle. This small difference defines the keyboard user's experience.

a11y.title: Beyond what you see
a11y.description: Follows the WAI-ARIA Menu Button pattern. Screen reader users get the same experience.
a11y.insight: Accessibility is not a finishing touch. role, aria-haspopup, and aria-expanded must be part of the design from the start so screen reader users experience the same flow.

attrs.haspopup: Declares that this button controls a popup menu
attrs.expanded: Communicates whether the menu is open to screen readers
attrs.controls: Links the button to the menu's DOM ID
attrs.menu: Identifies that child elements are menu items
attrs.disabled: Lets screen readers recognize disabled items
